title: [v5.8] fix automatic migration bug
body:
Fixes #28216


#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
This fixes a critical bug where the auto migration on first command after boot could leave things in a bad state where we have data in sqlite and boltdb at the same time. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28216
If you have encountered this on 5.8.0 there is no way to automatically recover. If you do not have persistent containers/pods/volumes (i.e. only using quadlets to mange them) then the easiest option is to delete the db.sql file (or maybe better move it elsewhere to store the config in case you need to recover something there) from our storage and reboot again with v5.8.1 to trigger the migration again. This time it should work correctly.
If you have problems with the migration please contact us and we try to help if possible.
```

comments.body:
Added No New Tests needed, I don't think I Can write a reasonable CI test case for the automatic parallel migration.
Given this will not go into main I think testing that manually once should be fine. I verified that against my reproducer here https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28216#issuecomment-4024951240.

Anyhow more testers appreciated for sure.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Tests are angry. But the code LGTM

yeah I need to think about it, the problem with switch states at the end is that if a user hard codes boltdb in the config/cli flag the code now switches over to sqlite as long as the sqlite db exists. That should not be the case. So I need to store the original config file value and respect that always.
